creator: jhibbits@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Brett from comment #5)

You have a TPM 1.2 device, not 2.0, given the messages and devices.  The TPM 1.2 driver creates a /dev/tpm device, while the TPM 2.0 driver creates a /dev/tpm0 device.  There is no /dev/acpi0, only /dev/acpi (there is only ever a single ACPI, so there's no need to give it a number as well).

I can't help with the bhyve pass-through, but I hope the above helps with some understanding.
